mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.09% indicates an extremely accommodative monetary policy stance, which reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and supports elevated valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return after any observation with FEDFUNDS ≤ 0.25% is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC is below 0.03 over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 7.0% signals a tightening labor market, which boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, resulting in positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE drops below 7.0%, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is negative in more than 50% of instances, or the average return is not statistically greater than zero.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts exceed 900 thousand units and the federal funds rate is below 1%, the combination of robust housing demand and cheap financing amplifies economic optimism, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If HOUST > 900 and FEDFUNDS < 1.0% but the forward 60-day SPX return is negative more than half the time, or the interaction sum in a regression is not significant at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: At the zero lower bound (FEDFUNDS ≤ 0.25%), the Federal Reserve cannot ease further, and any decline in the CPI signals deflation risk which depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS ≤ 0.25% and CPIAUCSL month-over-month declines, the forward 20-day SPX return is positive in more than 60% of cases, or the negative correlation has a rolling Spearman coefficient above -0.2.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measure residential investment and consumer confidence; a high level of starts continuously signals economic strength, supporting near-term equity gains.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return conditional on HOUST level is not positive on average over any 3-year period, or the correlation between HOUST level and next-5-day return is non-positive.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in retail and food services sales above 1% indicates strong consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate revenues and equities.
failure_condition: In periods when RSAFS MoM > 1%, the 20-day forward SPX return is negative in more than 40% of occurrences, or the average return is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated unemployment (above 7%) coupled with rising consumer prices (monthly CPI growth above 0.3%) creates a stagflationary environment, eroding corporate margins and consumer purchasing power, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: If UNRATE > 7% and CPI MoM > 0.3% but forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of episodes, or the joint signal does not exhibit negative average returns.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index above 100 indicates expanding manufacturing activity, which is a leading indicator of overall economic growth and positive short-term equity performance.
failure_condition: When INDPRO > 100, the 5-day forward SPX return is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) or the hit rate is below 55%.
